summary: R1 verdict CHANGES are resolved. (1) The phantom 'plan adr-coverage attribution path' is GONE; the plan-gate fix is now a REAL, tested requirement: a single shared normalization helper feeds the normalized domain-NAME set to checkADRCoverage AND checkADRCitations (both consume loadImpactedDomains at plan.go:124/735; verified they iterate impactedDomains BY NAME at :480/:520 with no file-attribution). R1's second AC explicitly tests the file-path Impacted-Domains spec passing the plan gates (no spurious adr-coverage-missing/adr-cite-irrelevant) and the helper's zero/multi-owner errors. (2) ParseSpec/internal/contextpack/spec.go is correctly OUT of the changed set: spec text states it is NOT touched in Impacted Domains, In-Scope ('NOT changed'), and Out-of-Scope; verified spec.go:84 still stores bare names and no requirement depends on it; the helper lives in the gate layer (internal/validate, where ownership.go already is) so there is no contextpack->validate dependency. (3) In-Scope file list is accurate and bounded: helper (validate/ownership), divergence.go (candidate set from normalized declared set, :142), plan.go (R1 consumption + R2 message :529 + R4 message :141), ownership.go (reuse, no behavior change), adr/parse.go (test-only, parser unchanged, confirmed parse.go accepts both Domain(s) forms), cmd/mindspec/adr.go (R3 root resolution), approve/spec.go (R4 scaffold), ADR-0032 doc amendment. Nothing listed exceeds a requirement; no requirement reaches beyond its bead. (4) Workflow-only Impacted Domain is HONEST: every changed source file (internal/validate/**, internal/ownership/**, internal/adr/**, internal/approve/**, cmd/**) is claimed by workflow's OWNERSHIP.yaml paths (verified globs present); context-system was correctly DROPPED because contextpack/spec.go is no longer touched (declaring it would be dishonest scope). The ADR-0032 doc amendment is a process artifact — verified isDocFile matches '.mindspec/docs/' and isProcessArtifact (divergence.go:183/260) skips it before attribution — so it needs no domain claim. (5) Decomposition stays clean across 4 beads: R1 (4ft2, substantive helper+two gates) / R2 (3d84, plan.go:529 message) / R3 (3cfr, cmd adr.go root + parse_test) / R4 (gpoq, approve scaffold + plan.go:141 message) touch different functions; R2's coverage-message edit does NOT depend on R1's helper (separate code path at :529 vs the consumption at :155). The new coupling from routing the plan gates through the shared helper is INTENTIONAL and bounded — it is the whole point of resolving #145+#147 at one source. (6) BLAST RADIUS BOUNDED: the only meta.Domains consumers are divergence.go:142 (R1 target), plan.go:735 loadImpactedDomains feeding the two plan gates (R1 target), contextpack/budgeter.go:180 and domain/show.go:85 (both read meta.Domains as a NAME list, untouched by normalization which preserves already-named entries verbatim), and docsync.go (does NOT read meta.Domains; already enumerates all domains via resolveDomains, so R1 brings divergence into LINE with doc-sync rather than diverging). Routing checkADRCoverage/checkADRCitations through the helper changes nothing beyond those two gates. APPROVE: creep removed (spec.go + phantom clause gone, plan-gate fix is a real tested requirement), blast radius bounded.
